---
title: |
  Can LLM clones predict a behavioral megastudy?
subtitle: "Preliminary results"
description: "Preliminary results: how well do LLM behavioral clones predict the effects of trust-in-climate-scientists interventions on humans."
date: 2026-05-20
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [simple, custom.scss]
    smaller: true
    slide-number: true
    progress: true
    chalkboard: true
    history: false
    include-after-body: section-label.html
    template-partials:
      - title-slide.html
    # incremental: true
    # code-link: true
image: ../images/mpg_logo.png
type: presentation
toc: false
---

```{r}
#| include: false
library(tidyverse)
library(gt)
library(here)
library(MetBrewer)
source(here("R/functions/plots.R"))   # plot_theme

res  <- function(name) readRDS(here("analysis/results", paste0(name, ".rds")))
meta <- res("_meta")
lab  <- meta$outcome_label_map

prelim <- sprintf("Preliminary — Human N = %s out of 22000",
  format(meta$human_n)
)

# Shared calibration-scatter (human estimate vs. LLM clone estimate).
scatter_calib <- function(df, xlab, ylab, color, facet, ncol = 3,
                          add_lm = TRUE) {
  p <- ggplot(df, aes(estimate_h, estimate_l, color = {{ color }})) +
    geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed",
                color = "grey60", linewidth = 0.4, alpha = 0.4) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted", color = "grey80") +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted", color = "grey80")
  if (add_lm)
    p <- p + geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, linewidth = 0.6)
  p +
    geom_point(size = 1.6, alpha = 0.65) +
    scale_color_met_d("Juarez") +
    facet_wrap(vars({{ facet }}), scales = "free", ncol = ncol) +
    labs(x = xlab, y = ylab, color = NULL) +
    plot_theme

}

scatter_calib <- function(df, xlab, ylab, color, facet, ncol = 3,
                          add_lm = TRUE,
                          add_cor = FALSE) {

  p <- ggplot(df, aes(estimate_h, estimate_l, color = {{ color }})) +
    geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed",
                color = "grey60", linewidth = 0.4, alpha = 0.4) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted", color = "grey80") +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dotted", color = "grey80")

  if (add_lm) {
    p <- p + geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = FALSE, linewidth = 0.6)
  }

  p <- p +
    geom_point(size = 1.6, alpha = 0.65) +
    scale_color_met_d("Juarez") +
    facet_wrap(vars({{ facet }}), scales = "free", ncol = ncol) +
    labs(x = xlab, y = ylab, color = NULL) +
    plot_theme

  if (add_cor) {

    cors <- df |>
      dplyr::group_by({{ facet }}) |>
      dplyr::summarise(
        r = cor(estimate_h, estimate_l, use = "complete.obs"),
        .groups = "drop"
      ) |>
      dplyr::mutate(label = paste0("r = ", round(r, 3)))

    p <- p +
      geom_text(
        data = cors,
        aes(x = Inf, y = Inf, label = label),
        inherit.aes = FALSE,
        hjust = 1.1,
        vjust = 1.2,
        size = 4
      )
  }

  p
}


# Source palette for density plots (Human + each model).
src_cols <- set_names(
  met.brewer("Juarez", n = length(meta$models) + 1),
  c("Human", meta$models)
)
```

::: {.callout-caution}
This talk has been given initially on very preliminary data (N ~ 3,500). All figures on the the slides will keep on updating with more data coming in. 

Currently, we're at N = `r prelim`
:::

---

# Why this project? 

---

- A planned megastudy to strentghen people's trust in climate scientists in the US
  - 20 interventions
  - 13 outcomes (including non-self-report)
  - 22,000 participants

- It's a thing for megastudies to let experts predict the results
  - which interventions work, and by how much? 

- We thought: why not let LLMs do the predictions?

# What’s the contribution

---

- Many studies predict outcomes of behavioral experiments with LLMs 

- But: 
  - many researcher degress of freedom in the simulations (prompts, LLM model choice etc.) and analyses (measures, statistical models, etc.)
  - these researcher degrees of feedom matter [@cumminsThreatAnalyticFlexibility2025b]

-> A fully pre-registered analysis of a large scale study seemed valuable

# Simulation

---

- Main idea: make behavioral clones and let them do the same survey as human participants

- We prompted LLMs with real persona data from the US General social survey
  - core demographics: `age`, `sex`, `race`, `education`, `income`, `household_size`, `rural_vs_urban`, `social_class`, `region`, 
  - other variables: `partisan_identity`, `religion`, `confidence_scientific_community`

- We preregistered analysis code and two fully generated clone data sets 
  - `google-gemini-2.5-flash-lite`
  - `openai-gpt-4o-mini`

:::{.notes}

Simulations on other models failed 
  - we used `openrouter` for making API requests
  - after a couple of thousand clones, we got blocked

We had to launch the big megastudy, so we couldn’t wait to fix that issue
:::

# Results

---

::: {.callout-warning}
`r prelim`
:::

--- 

## 1 · Average treatment effects

```{r}
#| label: fig-ate-1
#| fig-cap: "Association of ATEs between human and clone data. Each point = one intervention (out of 16) × one outcome (out of 13)"
#| out-width: "100%"

res("ate_pooled_pairs") |>
scatter_calib("Human ATE", "LLM clone ATE",
              color = NULL, facet = model, ncol = 2, add_lm = FALSE) 
```

## 1 · Average treatment effects

```{r}
#| label: fig-ate-2
#| fig-cap: "Association of ATEs between human and clone data. Each point = one intervention (out of 16) × one outcome (out of 13)"
#| out-width: "100%"


res("ate_pooled_pairs") |>
  scatter_calib("Human ATE", "LLM clone ATE",
                color = NULL, facet = model, ncol = 2, add_lm = TRUE, add_cor = TRUE)
```

## 1 · Average treatment effects

```{r}
#| label: fig-ate-3
#| fig-cap: "Association of ATEs between human and clone data. Each point = one intervention (out of 16) × one outcome (out of 13)"
#| out-width: "100%"
res("ate_pooled_pairs") |>
  scatter_calib("Human ATE", "LLM clone ATE",
                color = tier, facet = model, ncol = 2, add_lm = FALSE) +
  theme(legend.position = "right")
```

## 1 · Average treatment effects

```{r}
#| label: fig-ate-4
#| fig-cap: "Association of ATEs between human and clone data. Each point = one intervention (out of 16) × one outcome (out of 13)"
#| out-width: "100%"
res("ate_pooled_pairs") |>
  scatter_calib("Human ATE", "LLM clone ATE",
                color = tier, facet = model, ncol = 2, add_lm = TRUE)  +
  theme(legend.position = "right")
```

## 2 · Response distributions (control)

```{r}
#| label: fig-distribution
#| fig-cap: "Outcome distributions (control group) between human and clone data for some illustrative outcomes."
#| out-width: "100%"
res("dist_control_values") |>
  ggplot(aes(value, fill = source, color = source)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.30, linewidth = 0.4) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = src_cols) +
  scale_color_manual(values = src_cols) +
  facet_wrap(~ outcome_label, scales = "free") +
  labs(x = "Response", y = "Density", fill = NULL, color = NULL) +
  plot_theme + theme(legend.position = "top")
```

# Conclusion (preliminary)

---

- Prediction does not look great so far

- Maybe other, more "reasoning" models (e.g., gpt5, claude opus) do better? 

---

## Thank you!

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="50%"}

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="50%"}
![](../images/yara.jpg){width=100%}

![](../images/jon.jpg){width=100%}
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
![](../images/viktoria.jpg){width=100%}

![](../images/miriam.jpg){width=100%}
:::

::::

:::

::: {.column width="50%"}

**Project website**

[https://janpfander.github.io/llm_predictions_megastudy/](https://janpfander.github.io/llm_predictions_megastudy/)

![](../images/qr-code.png){width=100%}

:::

::::

# References

::: {#refs}
:::

# Backup

---

```{r}
#| label: tbl-rq1
#| tbl-cap: "RQ1 comparison metrics."

library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(gt)

wide <- res("ate_metrics") |>
  mutate(model = recode(model,
                        "Gemini-2.5-flash-lite" = "llm",
                        "GPT-4o-mini" = "h2")) |>
  select(model, tier, outcome_label,
         spearman_rho, pearson_r, rmse,
         directional_pct, inferential_pct, tost_pct) |>
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = model,
    values_from = c(spearman_rho, pearson_r, rmse,
                    directional_pct, inferential_pct, tost_pct),
    names_sep = "_"
  )


wide |>
  arrange(match(tier, c("All outcomes", "Primary", "Secondary", "Tertiary")),
          outcome_label) |>
  select(
    Tier = tier,
    Outcome = outcome_label,

    spearman_rho_llm, pearson_r_llm, rmse_llm,
    directional_pct_llm, inferential_pct_llm, tost_pct_llm,

    spearman_rho_h2, pearson_r_h2, rmse_h2,
    directional_pct_h2, inferential_pct_h2, tost_pct_h2
  ) |>

  gt(groupname_col = "Tier") |>

  tab_spanner(
    label = "Human 1 vs. Gemini",
    columns = ends_with("_llm")
  ) |>

  tab_spanner(
    label = "Human 1 vs. GPT-4o-mini",
    columns = ends_with("_h2")
  ) |>

  cols_label(
    spearman_rho_llm = "Spearman ρ", pearson_r_llm = "Pearson r", rmse_llm = "RMSE",
    directional_pct_llm = "Dir. (%)", inferential_pct_llm = "Infer. (%)", tost_pct_llm = "TOST (%)",

    spearman_rho_h2 = "Spearman ρ", pearson_r_h2 = "Pearson r", rmse_h2 = "RMSE",
    directional_pct_h2 = "Dir. (%)", inferential_pct_h2 = "Infer. (%)", tost_pct_h2 = "TOST (%)"
  ) |>

  fmt_number(
    columns = c(spearman_rho_llm, pearson_r_llm, rmse_llm,
                spearman_rho_h2, pearson_r_h2, rmse_h2),
    decimals = 3
  ) |>

  fmt_number(
    columns = c(directional_pct_llm, inferential_pct_llm, tost_pct_llm,
                directional_pct_h2, inferential_pct_h2, tost_pct_h2),
    decimals = 1
  ) |>

  sub_missing(columns = c(rmse_llm, rmse_h2), missing_text = "—") |>

  tab_style(
    style = cell_text(weight = "bold"),
    locations = cells_row_groups()
  ) |>
  tab_options(table.font.size = px(9))
```
